behaviors.description: Keyword-frequency analyzer assigns correct sentiment for reviews containing only configured positive/negative words
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: The failure mode is purely in one function's counting/threshold logic — no module handoff is involved. REAL-INFRA: No OS, network, or subprocess needed. LLM-DEP: The description explicitly states 'keyword-frequency analyzer with configurable thresholds' — this is a deterministic algorithm. A test can supply a config with known word lists and a known input and assert the output label. REJECTION-GATE: Integration was considered but rejected because the failure cannot hide at a module boundary — it lives entirely inside the analyzer's own logic.
behaviors.test_description: Instantiate the keyword-frequency analyzer with a small, explicit config (e.g., positives=['great','love'], negatives=['broken','awful'], thresholds=default). Pass a review containing only positive words and assert 'positive'; pass one with only negative words and assert 'negative'. Vary the word counts to probe threshold boundaries.

behaviors.description: Explanation analyzer produces reasoning that references specific phrases from the original review, not generic boilerplate
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP: The explanation analyzer 'interprets the review in context and produces natural language reasoning' — this is language generation. The failure mode is that the explanation is generic rather than grounded in review-specific phrases. A deterministic stub would always pass because you would program it to include the expected phrase; only the real model can actually fail to reference the phrase. MOCK-HIDE: Any mock hides the exact failure described. REJECTION-GATE: Integration was considered and rejected — there is no module-wiring failure here, only a question of what the real generative model produces. System was rejected because no real OS/network/subprocess behavior is the source of the failure.
behaviors.test_description: Feed the real explanation analyzer a review with distinctive, unusual phrases (e.g., 'the turquoise widget wobbles'). Run the analyzer and assert that the returned explanation contains at least one specific phrase or noun from the original review text rather than a fully generic template. Run several times to account for non-determinism.

behaviors.description: Sentiment score reflects the configured tie-breaking rule when a review contains mixed positive and negative keywords
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: The failure lives inside the tie-breaking branch of the same keyword-frequency analyzer — one function, one config path. REAL-INFRA: No external infrastructure. LLM-DEP: Tie-breaking is a deterministic rule (e.g., 'prefer negative', 'prefer positive', 'use count delta'). A controlled input with exactly equal positive and negative keyword counts will expose whether the configured rule fires correctly. REJECTION-GATE: Integration rejected — no other module's behavior affects whether the tie-breaking arithmetic is correct.
behaviors.test_description: Configure the analyzer with an explicit tie-breaking rule (e.g., ties go 'negative'). Submit a review with exactly equal counts of positive and negative keywords. Assert the returned label matches the configured rule. Repeat for each supported tie-breaking mode (positive-wins, negative-wins, neutral-on-tie).

behaviors.description: Explanation analyzer correctly identifies negative intent in a sarcastic review despite positive surface words
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP: Sarcasm detection requires pragmatic language understanding — recognizing that 'Great, another broken feature' is negative despite the word 'Great'. A deterministic stub cannot reproduce this failure; you would hard-code the stub to return 'negative' and the test always passes. The real failure appears only when the actual model processes the surface-positive phrasing. MOCK-HIDE: Any mock that returns a canned 'negative' explanation trivially passes. REJECTION-GATE: Integration rejected — there is no module boundary involved, only the model's own language understanding. System rejected — no OS or network behavior causes this failure.
behaviors.test_description: Feed the real explanation analyzer a canonical sarcastic review (e.g., 'Great, another broken feature'). Assert that the returned explanation classifies or describes the intent as negative and does not simply echo the surface-positive word at face value. Run multiple times; allow a pass rate threshold (e.g., ≥80%) to account for non-determinism.

behaviors.description: Both score and explanation are returned together in one response, with the explanation referencing the same sentiment direction as the score
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: The two outputs come from separate analyzers — this is a cross-module integration concern. But structural presence alone (both fields non-null) could be verified at Integration. The specific coherence requirement — 'explanation referencing the same sentiment direction' — requires verifying what the real LLM actually expresses, not just that a field exists. LLM-DEP: The explanation is generated text; a stub always returns a consistent explanation because you wrote it that way. Only the real model can actually contradict the deterministic score. MOCK-HIDE: Mocking the explanation analyzer hides the real failure: that the model's generated text may express a sentiment that conflicts with the keyword score. REJECTION-GATE: Integration was considered for the structural 'both fields present' sub-claim, but the behavior explicitly requires directional coherence of content, which forces Agentic. Workflow was rejected because only one model step (the explanation generator) is involved — this is one agentic step, not multiple in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Run the full service pipeline with the real explanation analyzer against reviews with clear, unambiguous sentiment (e.g., a strongly positive review). Assert: (1) the response contains both a score field and an explanation field, (2) the explanation's expressed sentiment direction (detectable by checking for sentiment-direction keywords in the explanation text) matches the label returned by the keyword-frequency score. Repeat for clearly negative inputs.
